manifest.caveat: Operational ranges are not individualized medical advice and low-confidence values are not proven physiological thresholds.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Use roughly 5-30 reps for hypertrophy when effort and technique are appropriate; default many compound lifts to 5-12 and stable isolations to 8-25.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Very light loads need closer proximity to failure; pain, cardio, grip, or balance can make a nominal range unproductive.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Start near 6-10 fractional hard sets per muscle weekly and adjust; many trained males may use 10-20, but returns diminish and muscle-specific thresholds are not established.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Ranges are operational and depend on effort, exercise, indirect work, session distribution, calories, sleep, and individual response.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Choose 1-3 weekly exposures per muscle to distribute volume; twice weekly is a convenient default, not a hypertrophy requirement.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Very high per-session volume may justify higher frequency; low-volume programs can work at once weekly.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Keep most productive sets around 0-4 RIR, generally closer for light-load/high-rep work and farther for high-skill compounds.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: RIR reporting is imprecise and failure is not required; the exact optimal RIR is unresolved.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Rest long enough to preserve output: often 2-5 minutes for demanding compounds and 1-3 minutes for stable isolations.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Hypertrophy differences beyond about 90 seconds are small and uncertain; time constraints and supersets change the tradeoff.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Count load, reps, lower RPE/higher RIR, better ROM, and better execution as progression only under comparable conditions and multi-session confirmation.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Deterministic thresholds are operational inferences; no universal plateau or regression cut point is validated.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Choose machines or free weights by target-muscle loading, comfort, skill, stability, and progression resolution; neither is inherently superior for hypertrophy.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Strength transfer remains modality- and task-specific.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Use the largest pain-free controlled ROM and include long-muscle-length loading where practical.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Long-length advantages are small/heterogeneous and muscle-specific; shortened partials can still be useful.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Target about 1.6-2.2 g/kg/day; in lean deficits consider about 1.8-2.4 g/kg body weight or 2.3-3.1 g/kg fat-free mass.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Higher intake is not automatically better and medical protein restrictions require clinician guidance.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Use a moderate deficit and slower loss when leaner or more advanced; preserve load exposure and reduce only low-value volume when recovery deteriorates.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: The approximately 500 kcal/day inflection is an average meta-regression estimate, not a universal safe limit.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Recomposition is most plausible in untrained, detrained, novice, higher-body-fat, or previously under-proteined males; advanced lean males should expect little.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Short-term DXA lean-mass changes include water/glycogen and do not equal contractile tissue.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Use the smallest surplus that supports a credible gain trend: roughly 0.25-0.5% body weight/week for novices, 0.15-0.3% for intermediates, and 0.1-0.25% for advanced males.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: These are practical review-based rates; trials do not establish an optimal surplus or universal muscle-to-fat ratio.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: A large volume reduction can often maintain muscle if meaningful load/effort exposure remains; start around 25-50% of recent productive volume and monitor.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Direct evidence in currently trained young males and during calorie restriction is sparse.

executive_summary.practical_recommendation: Use drop sets, rest-pause, and similar methods mainly for time efficiency or preference, not because they are proven superior.
executive_summary.important_exception_or_limitation: Method-specific evidence varies and fatigue can change set comparability.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: What repetition ranges can produce hypertrophy?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: A broad loading spectrum can produce hypertrophy when sets are sufficiently effortful; moderate loads are usually the most time-efficient default.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: Approximately 5-30 reps; 6-15 is the practical default for many exercises
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: A broad loading spectrum can produce hypertrophy when sets are sufficiently effortful; moderate loads are usually the most time-efficient default.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How should effort change with load?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: As loads become lighter and rep targets rise, sets generally need to finish closer to task failure to recruit and fatigue high-threshold motor units.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: Heavy/moderate: often 1-4 RIR; light/high-rep: often 0-2 RIR
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: As loads become lighter and rep targets rise, sets generally need to finish closer to task failure to recruit and fatigue high-threshold motor units.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Is momentary failure required?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Momentary muscular failure is not required for hypertrophy and can impose extra fatigue; it is more defensible selectively on stable isolation work than routinely on high-skill compounds.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Momentary muscular failure is not required for hypertrophy and can impose extra fatigue; it is more defensible selectively on stable isolation work than routinely on high-skill compounds.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How does weekly set volume affect hypertrophy?
evidence_conclusions.male_population: healthy adult males eligible for resistance training
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Hypertrophy generally increases with weekly set volume, but marginal returns diminish and individual recovery constrains useful dose.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: Start near 6-10 fractional hard sets; many trained males use roughly 10-20, adjusted by response
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: The numeric bands are operational starting ranges, not established per-muscle thresholds.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Hypertrophy generally increases with weekly set volume, but marginal returns diminish and individual recovery constrains useful dose.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How should indirect sets be counted?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Fractional counting is more defensible than either ignoring all indirect work or crediting every indirect set as a full direct set.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: Default 0.5 for a clearly loaded secondary mover; use 0-1 when exercise-specific evidence or technique warrants
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Fractional counting is more defensible than either ignoring all indirect work or crediting every indirect set as a full direct set.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How often should a muscle be trained?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Frequency is mainly a way to distribute weekly volume and preserve set quality; when volume is equated, hypertrophy differences are small.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: 1-3 sessions per muscle weekly; 2 is a convenient default
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Frequency is mainly a way to distribute weekly volume and preserve set quality; when volume is equated, hypertrophy differences are small.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How many hard sets should be performed per muscle in one session?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Spread large weekly volumes across sessions when per-session set quality, repetitions, or target-muscle limitation deteriorates.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: No validated universal per-session hypertrophy cap exists.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Spread large weekly volumes across sessions when per-session set quality, repetitions, or target-muscle limitation deteriorates.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How long should rest intervals be?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Rest long enough to preserve intended repetitions, load, range of motion, and target-muscle performance; compounds usually need longer rests than isolations.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: About 2-5 min for demanding compounds and 1-3 min for stable isolations
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Rest long enough to preserve intended repetitions, load, range of motion, and target-muscle performance; compounds usually need longer rests than isolations.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: What repetition duration supports hypertrophy?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Controlled repetitions across a broad range of durations can support hypertrophy; intentionally very slow repetitions are not superior.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Controlled repetitions across a broad range of durations can support hypertrophy; intentionally very slow repetitions are not superior.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Should full range of motion be the default?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Use the largest pain-free, controlled range that maintains the intended joint path and target-muscle loading; full ROM is a strong default, not an inviolable rule.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Use the largest pain-free, controlled range that maintains the intended joint path and target-muscle loading; full ROM is a strong default, not an inviolable rule.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Does training at long muscle lengths improve hypertrophy?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Including exercises or ranges that load a muscle at relatively long lengths is reasonable, but the average advantage appears small and muscle-specific.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Including exercises or ranges that load a muscle at relatively long lengths is reasonable, but the average advantage appears small and muscle-specific.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Which exercises should be performed first?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Place the highest-priority exercise or muscle earlier; hypertrophy appears similar across broad order strategies, while exercise-specific strength favors earlier placement.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Place the highest-priority exercise or muscle earlier; hypertrophy appears similar across broad order strategies, while exercise-specific strength favors earlier placement.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Are machines or free weights superior for hypertrophy?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Neither mode is inherently superior for hypertrophy; select for target-muscle loading, comfort, skill, stability, progression resolution, and transfer goals.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Neither mode is inherently superior for hypertrophy; select for target-muscle loading, comfort, skill, stability, progression resolution, and transfer goals.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Does periodization improve hypertrophy?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Varying load can support fatigue management and strength, but periodization has not clearly improved hypertrophy when volume is equated.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Varying load can support fatigue management and strength, but periodization has not clearly improved hypertrophy when volume is equated.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: What counts as progression?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Progression includes more repetitions or load with comparable execution and effort, the same performance at lower effort, or improved range and control at comparable load.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Progression includes more repetitions or load with comparable execution and effort, the same performance at lower effort, or improved range and control at comparable load.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: When should load increase?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Increase load only after the prescribed sets reach the top of their range with target RIR and standardized technique across the exercise-specific confirmation window.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Increase load only after the prescribed sets reach the top of their range with target RIR and standardized technique across the exercise-specific confirmation window.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How much training is needed to maintain muscle?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Muscle can often be maintained with substantially less volume than was used to build it if load/effort exposure is retained, but exact trained-male thresholds are unknown.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: Operational default: retain roughly 25-50% of recent productive sets and at least one exposure weekly
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Muscle can often be maintained with substantially less volume than was used to build it if load/effort exposure is retained, but exact trained-male thresholds are unknown.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How much protein supports hypertrophy?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Approximately 1.6 g/kg/day covers the average response plateau; up to about 2.2 g/kg/day is a conservative high-end target.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Approximately 1.6 g/kg/day covers the average response plateau; up to about 2.2 g/kg/day is a conservative high-end target.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How much protein supports retention during a cut?
evidence_conclusions.male_population: lean resistance-trained natural males in a deficit
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Protein needs may rise with leanness and deficit severity; use body weight for ordinary cuts and fat-free-mass scaling for very lean athletes.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: 1.8-2.4 g/kg body weight/day; review-based alternative 2.3-3.1 g/kg FFM/day for lean athletes
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Protein needs may rise with leanness and deficit severity; use body weight for ordinary cuts and fat-free-mass scaling for very lean athletes.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Does protein distribution matter?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Total daily protein is primary; distributing it across 3-5 meaningful feedings is a practical secondary consideration.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Total daily protein is primary; distributing it across 3-5 meaningful feedings is a practical secondary consideration.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How large should a deficit be when retaining or gaining muscle matters?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Avoid unnecessarily large sustained deficits; around 500 kcal/day was associated with prevented lean-mass gains on average, but retention remains possible with training and protein.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: Often 10-25% below maintenance; use the lower end when leaner or advanced
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Avoid unnecessarily large sustained deficits; around 500 kcal/day was associated with prevented lean-mass gains on average, but retention remains possible with training and protein.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: What rate of loss balances fat loss and retention?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Use a slower rate as the trainee becomes leaner, more advanced, or more performance-sensitive.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: About 0.5-1.0%/week for higher-fat or novice males; roughly 0.25-0.75%/week when lean/trained
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Use a slower rate as the trainee becomes leaner, more advanced, or more performance-sensitive.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Who is most likely to recomp?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Untrained, detrained, novice, higher-body-fat, and previously under-proteined males have the highest probability; advanced lean males should expect small or absent changes.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Untrained, detrained, novice, higher-body-fat, and previously under-proteined males have the highest probability; advanced lean males should expect small or absent changes.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: How large should a gaining surplus be?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Use the smallest surplus that produces a credible upward weight trend and productive training; larger surpluses increasingly add fat rather than guaranteed muscle.
evidence_conclusions.practical_range_text: Often 5-15%; advanced males should generally use the lower end
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Use the smallest surplus that produces a credible upward weight trend and productive training; larger surpluses increasingly add fat rather than guaranteed muscle.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: What rate of weight gain balances muscle and fat?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Target gain rate should decline with training age; treat scale change as a control signal, not a direct measure of muscle growth.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Target gain rate should decline with training age; treat scale change as a control signal, not a direct measure of muscle growth.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Should training volume drop during a deficit?
evidence_conclusions.male_population: resistance-trained natural males in a deficit
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Do not pre-emptively slash volume; retain enough hard work to preserve performance, then reduce low-value sets if recovery or performance deteriorates.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Do not pre-emptively slash volume; retain enough hard work to preserve performance, then reduce low-value sets if recovery or performance deteriorates.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Are advanced set systems superior?
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Advanced set systems are mainly time-efficiency or variety tools; they are not reliably superior to traditional sets for hypertrophy.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: Most syntheses combine sexes and training statuses; exercise- and muscle-specific data are sparse.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Advanced set systems are mainly time-efficiency or variety tools; they are not reliably superior to traditional sets for hypertrophy.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: What broad resistance-training prescription findings apply to healthy adults?
evidence_conclusions.male_population: healthy adult males represented indirectly within the broad ACSM position-stand population
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Progressive resistance training supports strength, hypertrophy, power, endurance, and physical function; multiple prescriptions work, and outcome-specific priorities should not be treated as one best program for everyone.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: This ACSM overview synthesizes 137 overlapping reviews and more than 30,000 participants without a separable aggregate male effect; overview cut points describe broad healthy-adult evidence, not male-specific universal thresholds.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Use the position stand to bound broad healthy-adult options, then apply goal, equipment, experience, adherence, and recovery constraints without claiming one universal prescription.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Does a precise RIR threshold optimize hypertrophy?
evidence_conclusions.male_population: resistance-trained and resistance-training-eligible adult males represented indirectly in mixed evidence
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Training closer to failure may increase hypertrophy on average, while 1-2 RIR can produce similar quadriceps growth to momentary failure; the exact dose-response and any optimal RIR threshold remain uncertain.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: The meta-regression used estimated RIR with exploratory models and modest fit; the direct trial was an eight-week, small mixed-sex, quadriceps-only study with non-separable male results.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Treat RIR bands as advisory product policy, preserve user effort and fatigue context, and never present one exact RIR as a validated universal optimizer.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Does a scheduled deload benefit adaptation?
evidence_conclusions.male_population: resistance-trained adult males represented indirectly in one mixed-sex trial
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: One week of complete training cessation in a nine-week program did not benefit hypertrophy and reduced strength gains relative to continuous training; evidence for reduced-load deload timing, triggers, and dose remains insufficient.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: This small mixed-sex trial studied complete cessation, not the reduced-volume or reduced-load deload algorithms commonly used in products, so it cannot establish a universal deload trigger.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Keep deload triggers and numerical reductions advisory and configurable; explain that they are product policy responding to repeated fatigue/performance evidence, not a directly validated schedule.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

evidence_conclusions.research_question: Can acute HRV evidence validate pre-session readiness changes?
evidence_conclusions.male_population: healthy adult males represented indirectly within heterogeneous acute resistance-exercise studies
evidence_conclusions.conclusion: Acute resistance exercise changes post-exercise heart-rate variability, but this does not establish that a pre-session HRV value should automatically increase, reduce, or cancel a planned workout.
evidence_conclusions.key_limitations: The review pooled heterogeneous acute outcomes and protocol moderators; causal readiness thresholds and longitudinal HRV-guided resistance-training algorithms remain unvalidated and uncertain.
evidence_conclusions.application_rule: Use HRV, when available, as optional advisory context alongside symptoms and performance; disclose missing data and do not enforce a hard training decision from HRV alone.
evidence_conclusions.exceptions: Pain, technique failure, medical contraindications, or recovery failure override the default.

muscle_group_recommendations.lengthened_position_considerations: Include at least one pain-free exercise that challenges the muscle at a relatively long length when practical; evidence is muscle-specific and does not justify painful ROM.
muscle_group_recommendations.indirect_set_contribution: Pressing contributes 1.0; close-grip or overhead pressing usually 0-0.5
muscle_group_recommendations.recovery_considerations: Adjust from the starting range using standardized performance, soreness that affects training, joint tolerance, sleep, and session quality rather than soreness alone.
muscle_group_recommendations.cutting_adjustment: Begin by retaining recent productive volume; reduce low-value sets 10-30% only if recovery or performance repeatedly deteriorates.
muscle_group_recommendations.recomp_adjustment: Use maintenance-to-moderate productive volume and prioritize repeatable progression.
muscle_group_recommendations.maintenance_adjustment: Operational estimate: approximately 25-50% of recent productive volume with retained effort/load exposure.
muscle_group_recommendations.bulking_adjustment: Add sets only after calories, technique, exercise selection, and recovery are adequate and performance has plateaued.
muscle_group_recommendations.notes: Set numbers are conservative operational starting ranges inferred from general dose-response evidence, not validated muscle-specific biological thresholds. Count hard working sets near the target RIR; exclude warm-ups.

exercise_database.joint_stress_considerations: Use a pain-free joint path and individual setup; persistent joint pain is a substitution trigger, not a progression target.
exercise_database.weekly_set_contribution: 1.0 to mg_chest_sternal; 0.5 default to clearly loaded secondary muscles
exercise_database.load_increase_criteria: All prescribed sets at 10 reps with target RIR and standardized execution for 2 session(s)
exercise_database.rep_increase_criteria: Add one repetition to the lowest-performing eligible set while technique and target RIR remain valid.
exercise_database.set_increase_criteria: Add one weekly set only after at least two weeks without meaningful progression, recovery is acceptable, and current sets retain quality.
exercise_database.regression_criteria: Comparable performance below recent median beyond expected noise for 2 sessions
exercise_database.deload_criteria: Two or more repeated regressions plus fatigue indicators, or widespread multi-exercise decline; do not deload from one noisy session.
exercise_database.technique_quality_criteria: Repeatable setup and joint path; no new compensatory momentum; target muscle remains limiting before pain, grip, cardio, or balance.
exercise_database.range_of_motion_criteria: Use the largest pain-free controlled ROM that preserves the intended joint path; ROM must be comparable before load/reps count as progression.
exercise_database.normal_performance_variability: Treat approximately 2-5% e1RM or one rep as possible session noise; use multiple sessions.
exercise_database.stall_definition: No improvement in reps, load, RIR, ROM, or execution across 3 comparable sessions after recovery checks
exercise_database.substitution_triggers: Persistent pain; target muscle not limiting; equipment increments too coarse; repeated technical breakdown; or no progress across a full mesocycle despite recovery and adherence.
exercise_database.suggested_substitutions: Use another exercise in the same movement pattern targeting the same primary muscle with lower joint or stability cost.
exercise_database.cutting_adjustment: Retain load exposure and target RIR; accept slower progression and reduce low-value sets before reducing effort on all sets.
exercise_database.bulking_adjustment: Do not force load jumps from calorie surplus alone; add volume only after performance and recovery support it.
exercise_database.notes: Exercise prescription is inferred mainly from movement-pattern evidence and fatigue/measurement constraints; it is not proof that this exercise is uniquely hypertrophic.

exercise_database.load_increase_criteria: All prescribed sets at 20 reps with target RIR and standardized execution for 1 session(s)
exercise_database.rep_increase_criteria: Add one repetition to the lowest-performing eligible set while technique and target RIR remain valid.
exercise_database.set_increase_criteria: Add one weekly set only after at least two weeks without meaningful progression, recovery is acceptable, and current sets retain quality.
exercise_database.regression_criteria: Comparable performance below recent median beyond expected noise for 2 sessions
exercise_database.deload_criteria: Two or more repeated regressions plus fatigue indicators, or widespread multi-exercise decline; do not deload from one noisy session.
exercise_database.technique_quality_criteria: Repeatable setup and joint path; no new compensatory momentum; target muscle remains limiting before pain, grip, cardio, or balance.
exercise_database.range_of_motion_criteria: Use the largest pain-free controlled ROM that preserves the intended joint path; ROM must be comparable before load/reps count as progression.
exercise_database.normal_performance_variability: One repetition or one small machine increment may be noise; use the defined confirmation window.
exercise_database.stall_definition: No improvement in reps, load, RIR, ROM, or execution across 3 comparable sessions after recovery checks
exercise_database.substitution_triggers: Persistent pain; target muscle not limiting; equipment increments too coarse; repeated technical breakdown; or no progress across a full mesocycle despite recovery and adherence.
exercise_database.suggested_substitutions: Use another exercise in the same movement pattern targeting the same primary muscle with lower joint or stability cost.
exercise_database.cutting_adjustment: Retain load exposure and target RIR; accept slower progression and reduce low-value sets before reducing effort on all sets.
exercise_database.bulking_adjustment: Do not force load jumps from calorie surplus alone; add volume only after performance and recovery support it.
exercise_database.notes: Exercise prescription is inferred mainly from movement-pattern evidence and fatigue/measurement constraints; it is not proof that this exercise is uniquely hypertrophic.

exercise_database.load_increase_criteria: All prescribed sets at 20 reps with target RIR and standardized execution for 1 session(s)
exercise_database.rep_increase_criteria: Add one repetition to the lowest-performing eligible set while technique and target RIR remain valid.
exercise_database.set_increase_criteria: Add one weekly set only after at least two weeks without meaningful progression, recovery is acceptable, and current sets retain quality.
exercise_database.regression_criteria: Comparable performance below recent median beyond expected noise for 2 sessions
exercise_database.deload_criteria: Two or more repeated regressions plus fatigue indicators, or widespread multi-exercise decline; do not deload from one noisy session.
exercise_database.technique_quality_criteria: Repeatable setup and joint path; no new compensatory momentum; target muscle remains limiting before pain, grip, cardio, or balance.
exercise_database.range_of_motion_criteria: Use the largest pain-free controlled ROM that preserves the intended joint path; ROM must be comparable before load/reps count as progression.
exercise_database.normal_performance_variability: One repetition or one small machine increment may be noise; use the defined confirmation window.
exercise_database.stall_definition: No improvement in reps, load, RIR, ROM, or execution across 3 comparable sessions after recovery checks
exercise_database.substitution_triggers: Persistent pain; target muscle not limiting; equipment increments too coarse; repeated technical breakdown; or no progress across a full mesocycle despite recovery and adherence.
exercise_database.suggested_substitutions: Use another exercise in the same movement pattern targeting the same primary muscle with lower joint or stability cost.
exercise_database.cutting_adjustment: Retain load exposure and target RIR; accept slower progression and reduce low-value sets before reducing effort on all sets.

exercise_database.notes: Exercise prescription is inferred mainly from movement-pattern evidence and fatigue/measurement constraints; it is not proof that this exercise is uniquely hypertrophic.

nutrition_strategies.male_population: trained natural males with moderate body fat
nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Allocate remaining calories to carbohydrate; place a meaningful portion around training when useful.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Retain productive volume; reduce 0-20% only if recovery declines.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Retain moderate/heavy load exposure and target RIR.
nutrition_strategies.cardio_guidance: Use the minimum cardio needed; increase gradually.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Prioritize sleep and manage rate of loss.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Primarily fat loss with better lean-mass retention than aggressive restriction
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: If loss exceeds target for 2 weeks or multi-lift performance declines, add calories or reduce fatigue.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Avoid aggressive restriction when already very lean, medically contraindicated, or showing low energy availability.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Carbohydrate based on preference and training performance after protein/fat targets.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Start at normal novice volume; avoid adding volume solely to burn calories.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Progress where possible; stable strength is not the ceiling in novices.
nutrition_strategies.cardio_guidance: Low-impact cardio may support the deficit.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Monitor joint tolerance, sleep, and adherence.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Substantial fat loss with reasonable recomposition potential
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Reduce deficit if loss exceeds 1%/week repeatedly or recovery deteriorates.
nutrition_strategies.recommended_duration: 8-24 weeks with maintenance breaks as needed
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Medical obesity or comorbidities may require clinician-led nutrition care.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Preserve carbohydrate around demanding sessions when possible.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Retain high-value sets; reduce 10-30% when repeated recovery issues occur.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Keep load exposure but avoid excessive failure work.
nutrition_strategies.cardio_guidance: Conservative low-impact cardio; avoid abrupt increases.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Watch mood, libido, sleep, resting heart rate, and persistent performance loss.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Slow fat loss with reduced but not eliminated lean-mass risk
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Stop or use maintenance if low-energy-availability symptoms or persistent regressions emerge.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Contest-level leanness carries health risks and is outside general fitness recommendations.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Adjust carbohydrate to activity and training quality.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Use productive or maintenance volume according to goal.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Progress normally when recovery supports it.
nutrition_strategies.cardio_guidance: Cardio for health and work capacity without compromising lifting.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Use stable weight to calibrate energy needs over at least 2 weeks.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Weight stability with performance recovery or slow recomp
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Adjust calories 3-5% after at least 2 weeks outside desired weight band.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Short-term water shifts can mimic energy imbalance.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Support progressive training with sufficient carbohydrate.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Use a recoverable novice program, not high-volume specialization.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Expect rep/load progression despite stable or falling scale weight.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Sleep 7-9 hours when feasible and maintain consistency.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Fat loss with simultaneous lean-mass gain is plausible
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: If waist and performance both stagnate 3-4 weeks, adjust calories or program after adherence audit.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Do not promise recomposition magnitude; measurement error is substantial.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Adequate carbohydrate to restore training performance.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Ramp from roughly 50-70% of prior volume over 2-4 weeks.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Use conservative loads initially and progress technique first.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Regain of prior muscle while maintaining or losing fat is plausible
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Slow the ramp if soreness affects next sessions or technique.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Prior injury or long layoff may require professional clearance.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Use carbohydrate to support high-quality training after protein/fat minimums.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Maintain productive volume; add sets only with evidence of need.
nutrition_strategies.cardio_guidance: Keep cardio stable for health and appetite management.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Track long trends; avoid reacting to glycogen/water noise.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Slow tissue gain with lower expected fat gain than aggressive surplus
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: If gain exceeds 0.25%/week for 2-3 weeks with rapid waist gain, reduce calories 3-5%.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: A surplus is not guaranteed to accelerate advanced hypertrophy.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.male_population: lean novice or intermediate natural males
nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Carbohydrate should generally provide the remaining energy to support training.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Use status-appropriate productive volume; more food does not justify unrecoverable sets.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Expect regular rep/load progress when technique is stable.
nutrition_strategies.cardio_guidance: Keep cardio consistent rather than eliminating it.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Monitor weight and waist using 2-3 week trends.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Muscle and strength gain with some fat gain
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Reduce surplus when rate exceeds status-specific target or waist rises without performance benefit.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Higher starting body fat may favor maintenance/recomposition before intentional surplus.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: No special carbohydrate allocation makes a large surplus muscle-specific.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: No evidence-based expectation of proportionally faster hypertrophy.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Monitor rapid waist and fat gain.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Likely disproportionate fat gain for most trained natural males
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Reduce calories promptly when gain exceeds target without exceptional context.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: May be appropriate only for clinically underweight males under professional guidance.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Distribute protein across 3-5 meals, often 0.3-0.55 g/kg per meal; total daily intake is primary.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Choose a pattern that supports adherence and sleep.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Convenient coverage of daily protein with uncertain incremental benefit from perfect distribution
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Simplify meal timing if it harms adherence; do not exceed calories merely to hit meal targets.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Kidney disease or prescribed protein restriction requires clinician guidance.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.male_population: lean resistance-trained natural males in prolonged deficits
nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Raise carbohydrate primarily while keeping protein stable; preserve the planned average deficit unless taking a maintenance break.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Maintain or slightly reduce volume according to fatigue.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Use restored carbohydrate to support performance, not forced PRs.
nutrition_strategies.cardio_guidance: Keep cardio stable or reduce briefly if fatigue-driven.
nutrition_strategies.recovery_guidance: Use for adherence, performance, or fatigue management rather than claimed metabolic reset.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Possible short-term performance/adherence benefit; body-composition superiority is uncertain
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Return to continuous dieting if breaks trigger loss of control or erase the intended deficit.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Evidence is small and mixed-sex; not required for successful fat loss.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.carbohydrate_guidance: Increase calories/carbohydrate if loss is above target and performance repeatedly declines.
nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: First remove low-value fatigue; do not remove all heavy exposure.
nutrition_strategies.training_intensity_adjustment: Treat stable strength as success; use multi-session trends.
nutrition_strategies.cardio_guidance: Reduce added cardio if it coincides with recovery decline.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Preserve muscle by correcting overly aggressive energy or fatigue load
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: If two or more lifts regress for 2+ comparable sessions and weight loss is above target, add 5-10% calories or reduce expenditure.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Pain or illness requires separate evaluation.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

nutrition_strategies.training_volume_adjustment: Do not apply natural-male dose or gain expectations as validated for enhanced males.
nutrition_strategies.expected_outcome: Population is explicitly separated; no combined recommendation
nutrition_strategies.adjustment_triggers: Do not infer from natural-male tables.
nutrition_strategies.contraindications_or_exceptions: Non-medical anabolic drug use has material health risks and is outside this database's prescriptive scope.
nutrition_strategies.notes: Ranges are application defaults, not individualized medical nutrition prescriptions.

evidence_gaps.research_question: What are true minimum effective, optimal, and maximum recoverable volumes for each male muscle?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: General dose-response evidence supports starting ranges, not exact muscle thresholds.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Few trials isolate one muscle, equate effort, count indirect work consistently, or sample advanced males.
evidence_gaps.conflicting_positions: Fixed per-muscle landmarks versus response-guided ranges
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Start 6-10 fractional hard sets and adjust from repeated performance/recovery data.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: False precision can cause needless volume escalation or undertraining.

evidence_gaps.research_question: What credit should indirect sets receive?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: A 0.5 credit is a query-friendly default, not a validated constant.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Contribution varies by technique, joint moment, ROM, individual anatomy, and proximity to failure.
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Use 1.0 primary, 0.5 clearly loaded secondary, and 0 when contribution is trivial; permit overrides.

evidence_gaps.research_question: What exact RIR maximizes hypertrophy at each load and exercise?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Closer is generally more important with lighter loads; momentary failure is not required.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Actual RIR is rarely measured accurately and protocols differ.
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Use 1-4 RIR for high-skill compounds and 0-3 for stable isolations; move closer with light loads.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Routine failure may add fatigue; staying too far may underdose light work.

evidence_gaps.research_question: What multi-session change proves meaningful progression?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Comparable improvement in load, reps, effort, ROM, or execution is meaningful; exact noise bands are exercise-specific.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Reliability studies do not validate app-wide decision thresholds.
evidence_gaps.conflicting_positions: Single-session PRs versus multi-session trend rules
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Require 1-3 confirmations depending on skill and variability; never ignore technique/ROM.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Overreacting to noise creates oscillating loads and unnecessary deloads.

evidence_gaps.research_question: Do scheduled deloads improve hypertrophy or retention?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Deloads are reasonable when accumulated fatigue is evidenced, but fixed 4-8 week schedules are not established.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Almost no direct longitudinal hypertrophy trials compare deload strategies.
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Use reactive multi-exercise regression and fatigue criteria; scheduled deload only by prior individual pattern.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Unnecessary deloads reduce stimulus; missed fatigue can increase injury or dropout risk.

evidence_gaps.research_question: What minimum volume maintains muscle in trained young men during a cut?
evidence_gaps.male_population: trained natural adult males in deficit
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Substantially reduced volume may maintain muscle if intensity/effort and protein remain adequate.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Key maintenance study included untrained young/older adults; deficit-specific trained trials test only limited contrasts.
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Start at 25-50% of prior productive volume only when maintenance is the goal; increase if performance or size trends decline.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Too-low defaults may lose tissue; too-high defaults waste recovery.

evidence_gaps.research_question: What surplus maximizes muscle-to-fat gain ratio?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Small surpluses are prudent; larger gain rates predict more fat but the optimal energy surplus is unknown.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Few controlled overfeeding trials in trained males and tissue measurement error is large.
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Use status-specific weight-gain rate and adjust from 2-3 week trends.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Large surpluses can cause unnecessary fat gain; no surplus may limit gain in some males.

evidence_gaps.research_question: Can an app estimate an individual's recomposition probability?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Training age, detraining, adiposity, protein, stimulus quality, and deficit size shift probability, but cannot yield a validated percentage.
evidence_gaps.conflicting_positions: Broad possibility claims versus strict novice-only claims
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Return qualitative high/moderate/low likelihood with uncertainty, never a precise probability.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: False promises can distort calorie and training choices.

evidence_gaps.research_question: Which named exercise is best for each subdivision?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Movement mechanics and limited regional hypertrophy trials support options, not universal rankings.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Few head-to-head longitudinal studies; individual anatomy and execution matter.
evidence_gaps.conflicting_positions: EMG-based rankings versus longitudinal outcome evidence
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Choose 1-3 complementary, pain-free exercises and judge by target limitation and progression.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: EMG-based certainty can select uncomfortable or poorly progressive exercises.

evidence_gaps.research_question: What sleep dose changes hypertrophy in trained males?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Adequate sleep is prudent for performance and health, but a precise hypertrophy threshold is not established.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Mostly acute sleep-loss or observational evidence; few longitudinal resistance-training trials.
evidence_gaps.conflicting_positions: Seven-hour threshold versus individual sleep need
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Flag repeated sleep below 7 hours as a recovery risk, not proof of impaired hypertrophy.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Treating a heuristic as causation can misclassify training response.

evidence_gaps.research_question: How should volume and effort differ in older trained males?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Older males can hypertrophy, but may need more conservative fatigue and joint-stress management.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Evidence often involves untrained or clinical older adults.
evidence_gaps.conflicting_positions: Same programming versus age-adjusted recovery defaults
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Use lower-end starting volume, 1-4 RIR, and gradual progression while monitoring function and pain.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Overgeneralizing young-male data may impair adherence or recovery.

evidence_gaps.research_question: How should recommendations differ for enhanced males?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: No combined recommendation is justified; pharmacology changes recovery, gain rate, risks, and clinical needs.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Ethical and methodological limitations; substance/dose heterogeneity.
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Keep enhanced status separate and return insufficient_evidence for dose optimization.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Combining populations can dangerously inflate natural-male expectations.

evidence_gaps.research_question: How many male participants support each mixed-sex meta-analysis?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Male applicability can be strong without a sex interaction, but exact pooled male counts are often not reported in abstracts.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Source papers vary in sex reporting and some reviews do not aggregate males separately.
evidence_gaps.conflicting_positions: Male-dominant inference versus male-specific effect claim
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Store null rather than invent a count; downgrade applicability unless male results are separable.

evidence_gaps.research_question: What male body-fat cutoffs should drive bulk/cut decisions?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: Body-fat context matters, but universal cutoff-based phase decisions are not validated.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Field body-fat estimates are noisy and health/risk varies continuously.
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Use broad contexts plus waist, health, preferences, and trajectory; never a single cutoff.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Measurement error can trigger inappropriate dieting or surplus.

evidence_gaps.research_question: Can EMG, hormones, soreness, or acute MPS rank programs?
evidence_gaps.current_best_interpretation: These measures may explain mechanisms but cannot by themselves prove superior long-term hypertrophy.
evidence_gaps.reason_for_uncertainty: Acute responses do not map reliably to chronic growth magnitude.
evidence_gaps.practical_default: Do not create hypertrophy rankings from acute markers alone.
evidence_gaps.risk_of_wrong_application: Misapplication can promote ineffective or harmful programming.

A completed working set for which the target muscle is meaningfully loaded and the set ends within the programmed RIR range; warm-ups excluded.
Non-standardized coaching term. In this database it means a hard set judged likely to provide useful stimulus; it is not a proven biological category.
A prescribed non-warm-up set used for progression or volume accounting.
Preparatory set intended to rehearse technique or readiness and excluded from hard-set volume and progression comparisons.
The highest-priority or highest-load working set in a session, defined by the program rather than assumed from load alone.
Working set performed after a top set with lower load and usually a higher repetition target.
Inability to complete the concentric phase through the prescribed ROM despite maximal effort.
Point at which another repetition would violate the predefined technique or ROM standard even if movement could continue.
Estimated number of additional repetitions possible before momentary muscular failure under the same technique and ROM.
Resistance-training effort scale where RPE 10 corresponds to 0 RIR, RPE 9 to about 1 RIR, with increasing uncertainty farther from failure.
Sum of credited hard working sets for a muscle across seven days; the application also stores direct and fractional indirect components.
Operational set dose intended to retain existing muscle; not a standardized or fixed physiological threshold.
Coaching term operationalized as a conservative starting dose likely to produce detectable progress in some trainees; not a validated universal threshold.
Range of hard-set doses associated with repeatable progress and acceptable recovery for the individual.
Region where additional sets are expected to add progressively smaller average benefit; it is not a hard cutoff.
Coaching term for the highest dose an individual can temporarily recover from; this database does not claim a validated numeric threshold.
Improvement in load, repetitions, effort, range, velocity, or technique under comparable conditions.
No improvement in any valid progression metric across the rule-defined comparable-session window after adherence and recovery checks.
Repeated decline beyond the exercise-specific noise band under valid comparable conditions.
Temporary planned reduction in training stress, usually through fewer sets and/or lower load/effort, used to dissipate fatigue.
Concurrent reduction in fat mass and increase in lean or muscle tissue over a defined measurement interval.
Average energy intake below estimated expenditure over a sufficiently long trend window.
Intake range associated with a stable multi-week body-weight trend, not a single exact daily number.
Average energy intake above estimated expenditure over a sufficiently long trend window.

change_log.reason_for_change: Created normalized evidence, application rules, exports, methodology, and validation package.
change_log.reviewer_notes: Version 1.0 is broad but not an exhaustive systematic review; low-confidence operational defaults are explicitly labeled.

change_log.previous_value: Cambered bench aliases were not discoverable.
change_log.new_value: Added one canonical cambered-barbell bench record with three aliases and normal chest mapping.
change_log.reason_for_change: Exercise discovery must evaluate eligible canonical library variations instead of silently omitting unrecognized aliases.
change_log.reviewer_notes: This is a movement-specific research default, not a claim that camber geometry guarantees superior hypertrophy.

change_log.previous_value: Primary 1.0 and blanket secondary 0.5 relationships generated from legacy exercise columns.
change_log.new_value: Taxonomy 2.0.0: direct, fractional, incidental, isometric, and unknown relationships with exercise-specific credit, fatigue, confidence, evidence, and review metadata.
change_log.reason_for_change: Hypertrophy volume and fatigue exposure require different semantics, and compound exercises require multi-muscle biomechanical classification.
change_log.reviewer_notes: Weights are transparent programming conventions, not proven physiological constants; low-confidence entries remain queued.

change_log.previous_value: Canonical subdivisions were projected through incomplete runtime name heuristics; the referenced cable woodchop exercise was absent.
change_log.new_value: Added a complete canonical-to-programming-family projection, a dynamic cable woodchop record, and family-aware relationship metadata while preserving every canonical muscle ID.
change_log.reason_for_change: Programming-family accounting must not double-count one exercise across anatomical subdivisions, and oblique programming needs at least one honest dynamic candidate.
change_log.reviewer_notes: The family projection is a product accounting layer, not an anatomical claim. Woodchop classification is movement-pattern inference with explicit uncertainty.

change_log.previous_value: Rules cited studies but did not trace through conclusion records, distinguish product policy from safety authority, or expose verified PubMed and PubMed Central identifiers.
change_log.new_value: Appended six primary evidence records, four scoped conclusions, an immediate pain blocker, bibliographic identifiers, conclusion traceability, authority, enforcement, and product-policy disclosure fields.
change_log.reason_for_change: Recommendation logic needs auditable evidence scope, explicit uncertainty, and a deterministic separation between hard safety constraints and configurable numerical heuristics.
change_log.reviewer_notes: Version 3.0.0 changes the public table schema. Historical persistent IDs remain unchanged; new records use append-only ID epochs. Numerical thresholds remain advisory product policy unless the rule is an allowlisted safety blocker.
